title: rubus foliosus var. illepidus
intro: Rubus foliosus var. illepidus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, characterized by its edible fruit and thimble-shaped receptacle. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rubus foliosus var. illepidus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation and pain, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as a tea, made by infusing the leaves or berries in hot water, or as a tincture, made by extracting the plant's active compounds in a solvent such as ethanol.
